channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: OpenAI turns down Musk's takeover bid
item.description: OpenAI’s board of directors has turned down Elon Musk’s $97.4 billion bid to buy the nonprofit that controls the artificial intelligence company. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Treasury watchdog office is auditing DOGE’s access to federal government’s payment system
item.description: The Treasury Department’s Office of Inspector General on Friday said it was launching an audit of the security controls for the federal government’s payment system, after Democratic senators raised red flags about the access provided to Trump aide Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency team
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Android 16 will bring these 5 upgrades to your phone – including one I can’t wait to try out
item.description: The latest Android 16 beta hints at a selection of new features that could make life easier and more creative for users of the world’s most popular mobile operating system.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Captain America: Brave New World ending explained: does Sam Wilson beat Red Hulk, is there a post-credits scene, are there any Marvel cameos, and more big questions answered
item.description: Captain America 4's ending sets up some interesting MCU story threads – here's what it means for the next batch of Marvel movies.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Your new Android notification superpower
item.description: It may seem like a paradox, but notifications are both the best and the worst part of owning an Android device. On the one hand, notifications let us stay on top of important incoming info — be it a critical Slack message, a personal family text, or an email from a high-priority client or colleague.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: BBVA permitirá gestionar por primera vez en España cuentas y tarjetas con IA
item.description: Blue, el asistente virtual de BBVA, arranca con hasta 150 consultas y operativas disponibles para clientes, lo que supone dar respuesta a más de 3.000 preguntas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Los CIO buscan innovación en IA más allá de los tres grandes proveedores de nube
item.description: AWS, Microsoft y Google pueden seguir dominando el mercado empresarial de la nube, pero una serie de proveedores de nube de segundo nivel están demostrando ser socios valiosos para organizaciones e innovadores con cargas de trabajo y casos de uso especializados, especialmente en la floreciente era d
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: CaixaBank impulsa el Plan Cosmos con una inyección de 5.000 millones para su transformación digital
item.description: CaixaBank pisa el acelerador de su plan de transformación digital contemplado en la hoja de ruta estratégica de la compañía definida para el periodo 2025-2027. Así lo ha avanzado Expansión.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The scent of the mummy. Research discovers ancient Egyptian remains smell nice
item.description: Researchers who sniffed mummified remains in the name of science were greeted more with a blessing than a curse.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk’s Tesla fined for alleged ‘serious’ failure to protect Fremont plant workers from excessive heat
item.description: The regulatory action comes as Tesla CEO Musk works as a special government employee for President Donald Trump to dramatically -- and critics say illegally -- slash federal government spending and programs, including in agencies responsible for regulating the operations of Tesla.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why asteroid 2024 YR4 is unlikely to hit Earth in 2032 and how scientists keep track
item.description: Scientists say the threat of a newly discovered asteroid has risen slightly in the past few weeks, as the world's telescopes rush to track its course.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Adobe Firefly expands with ‘commercially safe’ video generator
item.description: Adobe has released a video generator in public beta in its generative AI (genAI) tool, Adobe Firefly. The company calls the tool the first “commercially safe” video generator on the market.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Plans to spend $400 million on ‘Armored Teslas’ vanish from State Department procurement list
item.description: Elon Musk’s unprecedented role in Donald Trump’s administration as overseer of government spending has raised questions about how he might police himself when one of his companies competes for official contracts.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Juniper unveils EX4000 access switches to simplify enterprise network operations
item.description: Juniper has expanded its access layer options with a new switch family it says will help customers use automation and AI-based support to streamline enterprise operations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fox Host Criticizes Elon Musk Over Lack of Transparency
item.description: Jessica Tarlov, a host on Fox News, criticized billionaire Elon Musk for lacking transparency in his efforts to reform the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Italian government denies it spied on journalists and migrant activists using Paragon spyware
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Italian government denied Wednesday that it had spied on journalists and migrant activists using spyware but said it would cooperate with an investigation into “vulnerabilities” after at least seven Italian cellphones were apparently hacked with military-grade surveillance technology
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CISPE raises red alert over Broadcom’s VMware licensing changes
item.description: The European Cloud Competition Observatory (ECCO), a monitoring body established by the association of Cloud Infrastructure Services Providers in Europe (CISPE) as a way to keep cloud licensing practices fair in the region, this week came down hard on Broadcom for its failure to do so.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Memo: Musk rivals Trump as most controversial figure on political stage
item.description: President Trump may be only the second most controversial person in his own administration. Three weeks into Trump’s second term, Elon Musk has taken center stage — as big of a villain for liberals and Trump critics as he is a hero for MAGA conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, The Hill

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: HPE expands ProLiant server portfolio, boosts AI and security features
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) added eight new servers to its ProLiant Gen 12 server portfolio, bringing advanced security and AI-optimization features to enterprise customers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: VP calls for ideology-free AI
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Snowflake announces preview of Cortex Agent APIs to power enterprise data intelligence
item.description: Snowflake on Wednesday announced the public preview of Cortex Agents, a set of APIs built on top of the Snowflake Intelligence platform, a low-code offering that was first launched in November at Build, the company’s annual developer conference.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Don’t use public ASP.NET keys (duh), Microsoft warns
item.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence in December observed a “threat actor” using a publicly available ASP.NET machine key to inject malicious code and fetch the Godzilla post-exploitation framework, a “backdoor” web shell used by intruders to execute commands and manipulate files.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Don't fall for that sneaky iCloud storage alert in your inbox
item.description: Beware of iCloud scams using urgent messages. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you spot the red flags to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A new interpreter in Python 3.14 delivers a free speed boost
item.description: Python 3.14, due out later this year, is set to receive a new type of interpreter that can boost performance by up to 30% with no changes to existing code. The CPython 3.14 change log describes the feature as “a new type of interpreter based on tail calls.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: FPGAs lose luster in genAI era
item.description: In a world gone mad over GPUs for AI acceleration, FPGA processors are seeing limited adoption in the data center for genAI workloads, with their primary use still in embedded markets and edge AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Top 20 apps tracking you every day
item.description: The apps collecting the most data about you are among the most widely used. Kurt the CyberGuy takes a look at 20 of these apps and how you can protect personal information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Is PSN still down? PlayStation Network addressing ongoing outage
item.description: Thousands of users have reported outages with the PlayStation Network since Friday evening but as of Saturday morning things still didn't seem back normal.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, NewsNation
